## LLM Dimensions (60% of score)

| Dimension | Wt | v2 | v1 | General |
|-----------|:--:|:--:|:--:|:-------:|

| Data Grounding & Source Citation | 16% | 5/5 | 4/5 | 3/5 |
| Quantitative Accuracy & Calculation Discipline | 16% | 5/5 | 4/5 | 1/5 |
| Output Structure Completeness | 12% | 5/5 | 4/5 | 1/5 |
| Action Specificity           | 12% | 5/5 | 3/5 | 1/5 |
| Data Integrity & Gap Disclosure | 12% | 5/5 | 4/5 | 2/5 |
| Trend & Continuity           | 12% | 5/5 | 2/5 | 2/5 |


### Reasoning

**v2 Agent:** The v2 brief demonstrates the highest quality across all dimensions: every sector row carries a named direction, urgency, and quantified impact traceable to a named source; all four required calculations are present with explicit ±25bps and ±50bps EIBOR scenarios, USD-to-AED conversions at the fixed 3.6725 rate, and clearly disclosed estimation bases for any non-live inputs. Streak language is precise and sourced from state.json (5th consecutive Fed hold, 214 consecutive CBUAE unchanged days, 4th consecutive rising Brent session), satisfying the Trend & Continuity dimension fully. Action flags name specific EDB teams, concrete trigger thresholds, and reassessment milestones, and every stale or estimated input is flagged with its vintage and estimation method.

**v1 Agent:** The v1 brief is well-structured and covers all five EDB sectors with directional and urgency labels, and the key AED number (AED 1,448mn feedstock impact) is prominently displayed and traceable to named sources. Data integrity is handled responsibly — stale FRED data is flagged with vintage, the OPEC basket null return is disclosed, and the Operation 300bn methodology correction is explicitly noted. However, action flags lack the specificity of named EDB teams and concrete trigger thresholds, and as a structurally capped brief without state.json access it cannot achieve above 2/5 on Trend & Continuity regardless of prose quality.

**General Agent:** The general brief reads as a competent narrative summary but fails on nearly every structured dimension required by EDB's framework: there is no sector impact matrix, no EIBOR sensitivity calculation block, no explicit USD-to-AED conversion, no action flags with named teams or trigger conditions, and no 72-hour watchlist. Source citations are present but inconsistently linked to specific numbers, and the brief conflates a June 17 date with July 13 market data without flagging the inconsistency. Without state.json access it is structurally capped at 2/5 for Trend & Continuity, and the absence of all required calculation outputs caps Quantitative Accuracy at 1/5.
